description: 2 IMMIGRATION Be WHAT WE OWE TO THE INFLUX OF FOR EIGNERS WHAT OUR POPULATION WOULD HAVE BEEN HAD IMMIGRATION STOPPED AT THE CLOSE OF ANY DECADE SINCE 1790HOW OUR MIL ITARY POWER HAS BEEN INCREASED AND WEALTH AND PROSPERITY INSURED BY THE MOVEMENT TOWARD OUR SHORES The growth of the United States in popnlation and resources is one of the grandest facts of the Nine teenth Century Nowhere in history do we find a parallel for this stupendous development Doubtless much of it is due to our admirable form of govern ment but itis not less certain that a considerable portion of the result is to be ascribed to the influx of foreigners secking here permanent homes for them selves and their children Comparatively few Amer jcan citizens can trace their lineage back to the for mation of the Union without meeting some immi grant ancestor Indeed hardly a village can be found in the land that does not contain among its in habitants one or more persons of alien birth while the population in numerous other localities even in whole States includes a large proportion of the same class x To obtain an approximate conception of the fruits
